Protecting Film Creators’ Rights in Kenya”

The Filmmakers Rights Achievers of Kenya (FRAK) is a Collective Management Organization (CMO) dedicated to safeguarding the rights and welfare of film creators and audiovisual producers across Kenya

Contact Us

About Filmmakers Rights Achievers of Kenya (FRAK)

(FRAK) is a Collective Management Organization (CMO) established under the Copyright Act, Cap 130 of the Laws of Kenya.

We represent film creators, producers, and Audiovisual producers by managing their copyrights, collecting royalties, and safeguarding their economic rights.

FRAK ensures that every Filmmaker and Audiovisual producers in Kenya earns fair compensation when their works are broadcasted, streamed, or used in public. Through transparency, advocacy, and collaboration with key regulators like KECOBO and KFCB, FRAK is driving the growth and professionalization of Kenya’s film industry.

Contact Us

What FRAK DOES

We protect and promote the rights of filmmakers and audiovisual creators across Kenya

1️⃣ Rights & Royalties Management

We advocate for Filmmakers and Audiovisual producers rights and fair compensation.

4️⃣ Member Welfare & Industry Growth

We train, support, and advocate for the growth of Kenya’s film creators.

2️⃣ Licensing & Compliance

We manage licensing to protect creative works.

3️⃣ Copyright Protection & Legal Support

Ensuring filmmakers receive due royalties.

Contact Us

WHY FRAK MATTERS

FRAK exists to protect the heartbeat of Kenya’s film industry — its creators.
By standing for fair pay, legal protection, and recognition, FRAK ensures filmmakers and audio- visual producers can earn from their work and build sustainable creative careers.

FRAK gives a voice to Kenya’s filmmakers — ensuring their stories and rights are valued.

ROBERT NYAGA

Role: Consultative Director in charge of Administration and key advisor ensuring welfare and inclusivity within FRAK
Focus: Member welfare and community engagement.

EVANS KARANJA

Role: Founding and Consultative Director and strategic overseer guiding FRAK's long-term vision
Focus: Rights holding management, partnerships and creative development

Board of Directors

FRAK's board consists of 7 Directors: 2 consultative directors, 2 Audiovisual producers from music sector,2 Film producers and 1 Animation creator
